failure_modes.text: A single plausible frame can hide important behavior or edge cases when the team never tests materially different cuts of the same problem.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when one frame is clearly under-explaining the problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Force at least one structurally different cut: after the primary analysis, require a pass using a different framework, dataset, or assumption set to surface what the first frame hid.

failure_modes.text: Expert pattern recognition can become misleading when familiar schemas are reused without contrast against alternative views.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit pattern reuse: when expert schemas are applied, check whether the current case has features that differ from the template and whether those differences are material.

failure_modes.text: Frame variety can turn into aimless wandering when teams keep adding examples and lenses without using them to sharpen the decision.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when variety becomes undisciplined wandering.
failure_modes.mitigation: Set a convergence rule: after introducing varied perspectives, define when the team must synthesize and decide, so frame variety does not become aimless exploration.

premortem_questions.text: What materially different lens have we not yet tested on this problem structure?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What alternative frames have we explicitly tested?

premortem_questions.text: Which current frame mostly reflects our home discipline instead of the problem’s actual structure?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If the team has only used frameworks derived from its primary discipline, the perspective is likely too narrow.

premortem_questions.text: What specific output must this frame contrast produce, so we know whether the added variety is helping the decision?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What specific output is required from this analysis to move the solution forward?

heuristics.text: Test at least two or three materially different frames before locking in the operating story.
heuristics.source_quote: Best when teams need contrast before commitment.

heuristics.text: Use contrasting examples, counterexamples, and alternative cuts so learning transfers beyond the first case.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when learning must transfer across situations.

heuristics.text: Prune frame exploration once it has exposed the highest-value contrast instead of continuing for completeness theater.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on frame contrast, transfer, and disciplined pruning rather than on generic multidisciplinary enthusiasm.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor brainstorming or graph-walk sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on contrasted frame testing.
open_questions: Some anti-pattern language could also support communication or teaching models, but the intervention file stays on decision framing.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on synthesis, feynman-technique, and structured problem decomposition.
antagonists: The source is strongest against anchoring, specialization drift, and confirmation-bias in frame selection.
structured_tensions: The model contains explicit tension lines around anchoring and confirmation-bias that cleanly belong in Wave 3.
